reasoning: The FRED data for commercial bank deposits shows a steady average upward drift over the last year of ~16 billion USD per week, with a week-to-week standard deviation of ~59 billion USD. Thus, while purely mathematical extrapolation of a random walk with positive drift would imply the probability of an increase approaches 1.0 over a longer horizon, I am adhering to the direct prompt instruction and decreasing my probabilities toward 50% for the progressively more distant dates.
